title: Admin Layout Overhaul: Collapsible Drawer + Command Center + Split Focus
description:
Restructure admin page layouts with three key patterns:
1. Collapsible Drawer sidebar for ALL pages (replaces current fixed sidebar)
2. Command Center dashboard for admin home page (/admin)
3. Split Focus master-detail for employee and student list/edit views

This improves workflow efficiency by:
- Maximizing content width with collapsible nav
- Giving admins a bird's-eye overview dashboard
- Enabling rapid cycling through records without losing list context

title: Phase 1: CollapsibleSidebar component
description:
Create a new CollapsibleSidebar component to replace the current SidebarNav.

Current state:
- SidebarNav (app/ui/components/SidebarNav.tsx) renders a fixed 256px sidebar on desktop, Sheet on mobile
- Used by AdminShell and AppShell

New component behavior:
- Default state: collapsed icon-only rail (~48px wide)
- Hover OR click pin: expands to full width (~220px) with labels
- Smooth CSS transition (width + opacity on labels)
- Active route indicator (left border accent)
- Grouped sections with dividers (same groups API as current SidebarNav)
- Mobile: same Sheet behavior as today (no change)
- Pin/unpin toggle button at bottom of sidebar to lock expanded state
- Persist pinned state in localStorage

Files to create/modify:
- NEW: app/ui/components/CollapsibleSidebar.tsx
- MODIFY: app/ui/shells/AdminShell.tsx (swap SidebarNav for CollapsibleSidebar)
- MODIFY: app/ui/shells/AppShell.tsx (swap SidebarNav for CollapsibleSidebar)
- Keep SidebarNav around for backward compat (can deprecate later)

Props interface should match current SidebarNav groups API for drop-in replacement.

title: Phase 2: Admin Command Center dashboard
description:
Create an admin home/dashboard page at /admin (index route) with a Command Center layout.

Current state:
- No /admin index route exists. Hitting /admin shows nothing (Outlet with no match)
- Admins navigate directly to /admin/employees or /admin/students

New page: app/routes/admin/index.tsx

Loader fetches aggregate stats:
- Active/inactive employee counts
- Active/inactive student counts
- Expiring ID count (within 30 days)
- Expired ID count
- Missing photo counts (employees + students)
- Last sync timestamps (staff + student)

UI structure:
1. Top stat cards row (4 cards): Active Employees, Expiring IDs, Missing Photos, Last Sync
   - Each card is clickable, links to the relevant section
   - Color-coded: green/amber/red/blue borders
2. Section cards row (3 cards): Employee List, Student List, Card Designs
   - Each links to the section with icon + brief description
3. Needs Attention table: employees/students with expired or expiring IDs
   - Compact table showing name, type, expiration date, status
   - Links to detail page on row click

Dependencies: Phase 1 (sidebar must be in place first so dashboard renders in new shell)

Files:
- NEW: app/routes/admin/index.tsx

title: Phase 3: Split Focus layout for Employee list + detail
description:
Convert the employee list + detail pages into a persistent master-detail (split focus) layout.

Current state:
- /admin/employees renders a full-page table with search/filters
- Clicking a row opens /admin/employees/:id as a separate full page
- The EmployeeQuickViewDrawer exists but is a temporary Sheet overlay, not persistent
- Going back to the list loses scroll position and selection state

Target architecture:
Use React Router nested routes to render the list and detail side by side.

Option A (preferred): Layout route approach
- app/routes/admin/employees.tsx becomes a LAYOUT route that renders the split
- Left panel: scrollable filterable list (derived from current index.tsx)
- Right panel: <Outlet /> renders the detail ($employeeId.tsx) or empty state
- When no employee selected, right panel shows a prompt to select one

Option B: Client-side split within index.tsx
- Keep current route structure but fetch detail data client-side
- More complex, less aligned with React Router conventions

Implementation details (Option A):
1. NEW: app/routes/admin/employees.tsx (layout route with split panels)
   - Left panel (w-80 to w-96, fixed, border-r, overflow-y-auto):
     * Search input at top (sticky)
     * Scrollable employee list with compact cards
     * Active item highlighted with left-border accent
     * Each item shows: name, status badge, photo indicator, expiration warning
   - Right panel (flex-1, overflow-y-auto):
     * <Outlet /> for nested routes
     * Empty state when no employee selected
   - Both panels scroll independently

2. MODIFY: app/routes/admin/employees/index.tsx
   - Becomes the "no employee selected" empty state
   - Or redirects to first employee

3. KEEP: app/routes/admin/employees/$employeeId.tsx
   - Content stays the same but renders inside the right panel
   - Remove "Back to Employees" link (list is always visible)
   - May need to adjust widths (was full-page, now constrained)

4. Mobile behavior:
   - Below md breakpoint: list is full-width, detail is full-width
   - Use URL-based navigation (standard mobile pattern)
   - No split on mobile

Key considerations:
- Preserve all existing functionality (search, filters, bulk actions, sync)
- List should update when detail actions change data (revalidation)
- Selected employee should be visually highlighted in the list
- URL should reflect selected employee (/admin/employees/abc123)
- Search params (search, status, photo filters) preserved in list panel

title: Phase 4: Split Focus layout for Student list + detail
description:
Mirror the employee split focus layout for students.

Same pattern as Phase 3 but for the student routes:

1. NEW: app/routes/admin/students.tsx (layout route with split panels)
   - Left panel: scrollable filterable student list
   - Right panel: <Outlet /> for student detail or empty state
   - Items show: name, grade, status badge, photo indicator

2. MODIFY: app/routes/admin/students/index.tsx
   - Becomes empty state / no-selection view

3. KEEP: app/routes/admin/students/$studentId.tsx
   - Renders in right panel, remove "Back to Students" link
   - Adjust widths for constrained panel

4. Mobile: same as employees — full-width list, full-width detail

This depends on Phase 3 because:
- We can copy the pattern directly once employees are working
- Shared components (list item, empty state) can be extracted during Phase 3
- Reduces duplication risk by doing employees first as the template

Potential shared extractions after both phases:
- SplitFocusLayout component (left/right panels with responsive behavior)
- CompactListItem component (avatar, name, badges row)
- EmptyDetailState component (icon + prompt text)

title: Phase 5: Polish, cleanup, and responsive QA
description:
Final polish pass after all layout changes are in place.

Tasks:
1. Remove the layout-explorations route and component (served its purpose)
2. Remove or deprecate old SidebarNav if fully replaced
3. Responsive testing:
   - Mobile (<640px): verify Sheet nav, full-width pages, sticky action bars
   - Tablet (640-1024px): verify sidebar collapse behavior
   - Desktop (>1024px): verify split focus panels, dashboard grid
4. Dark mode pass: ensure all new components respect dark theme tokens
5. Keyboard accessibility: sidebar expand/collapse, list navigation, focus management
6. Performance check: no layout shift on sidebar expand, smooth transitions
7. Update AdminShell nav items if any routes changed
8. Clean up any TODO comments from implementation phases

title: Make School-ID deployable by any school
description: Transform the JPG-specific ID system into a generic school ID system that any FACTS SIS school can deploy. Includes a first-run setup wizard, removal of hardcoded JPG references, README with Fly.io deployment guide, and optional Google Workspace integration.

title: Remove all hardcoded JPG/John Paul references
description: Replace all hardcoded 'JPG', 'John Paul the Great Academy', addresses, phone numbers, and school-specific defaults throughout the codebase with values pulled from brand.ts (which itself reads from env vars or DB settings). Files to update: app/ui/brand.ts, app/root.tsx, app/routes/admin/index.tsx, app/routes/admin/print.tsx, app/routes/admin/signatures/templates.tsx, app/utils/pdf-card-designs.tsx (hardcoded NAVY/MAROON/CREAM colors, address, phone), public/site.webmanifest, tests. brand.ts defaults should be generic ('School ID System', 'Your School').
close_reason: Removed all hardcoded JPG/John Paul references, addresses, phone numbers. All values now come from SchoolConfig/BrandingConfig.

title: Remove JPG-specific static assets, make logo uploadable
description: Remove public/img/jpg-logo.svg, jpg-logo.png, JPG-Crest.png from the repo. Replace with generic placeholder assets. The wizard and admin settings allow uploading school logo and crest to object storage. Update all references to use the uploaded URL from SchoolConfig instead of static paths. The verification page and ID cards should pull logo from storage.

title: Add SchoolConfig model to DB (SystemSetting or new table)
description: Move school configuration from env vars into the database so the setup wizard can write them. Use the existing SystemSetting key/value table or create a SchoolConfig table. Keys needed: school_name, school_short_name, school_address_line1, school_address_line2, school_phone, school_email_domain, school_logo_url, school_crest_url, primary_color, secondary_color, accent_color, academic_year_format. Create a getSchoolConfig() server utility that reads from DB with env var fallbacks. Update branding.server.ts to use it.
close_reason: Created school-config.server.ts with SchoolConfig interface, getSchoolConfig() with DB+env fallback, cache, and setter utilities. Updated branding.server.ts to use it.

title: Build first-run setup wizard (expand install.tsx)
description:
Expand the existing install.tsx into a multi-step setup wizard. The wizard should only appear when no admin user exists (current behavior). Steps:

1. **Welcome** - Explains what School ID does, what you'll need to set up
2. **School Branding** - School name, short name, address, phone, email domain, upload logo/crest, pick primary/secondary/accent colors (with live preview of an ID card)
3. **Admin Account** - Username, email, password (not just email like current). Creates the first admin user with password auth.
4. **FACTS SIS Connection** - FACTS API key, subscription key, base URL. Test Connection button that hits the FACTS API and confirms it works. Explain where to get these keys.
5. **Google Workspace (Optional)** - Toggle on/off. If on: Google OAuth client ID/secret/redirect URI for user login, Google service account email/key/admin email for photo sync and Gmail signatures. Explain what each feature does and where to get credentials. Link to Google Cloud Console setup docs.
6. **Object Storage** - Tigris/S3 credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, SECRET, REGION, ENDPOINT, BUCKET). Test connection button.
7. **Photo Sources** - Explain that photos come from: (a) FACTS SIS profile photos (auto-synced), (b) Admin manual upload, (c) Optionally pushed to Google Workspace. Let them understand the flow.
8. **Review & Complete** - Summary of all settings, Finish button that writes everything to DB/SystemSetting.

Technical: Use React Router form actions, store config in SystemSetting table. Secrets (API keys, etc.) should be stored encrypted or written to .env on the server. Redirect to /admin after completion.

title: Make Google features conditional (feature flags)
description: Google Workspace integration (OAuth login, Gmail signature push, photo sync to Google) should be fully optional. If the admin didn't configure Google in the wizard, these features should be hidden from the UI entirely. Add a 'google_enabled' flag to SystemSetting. Conditionally show/hide: Google OAuth login button on auth pages, Gmail signature management in admin, Google photo sync options. The Google provider in connections.server.ts already gracefully handles missing config - extend this pattern to the UI layer.

title: PDF card designs use SchoolConfig instead of hardcoded values
description: app/utils/pdf-card-designs.tsx has hardcoded NAVY (#1B2A4A), MAROON (#8B1A2B), CREAM (#F5F0E8) colors and hardcoded school address/phone. Update all 12 designs to pull colors from BrandingConfig (which now reads from SchoolConfig DB). Replace hardcoded address constants (SCHOOL_ADDRESS_LINE1, SCHOOL_ADDRESS_LINE2, SCHOOL_PHONE) with values from SchoolConfig. The card preview in the wizard should show these colors live.
close_reason: All 12 PDF card designs and HTML preview designs now read colors and contact info from BrandingConfig instead of hardcoded constants.

title: Admin settings page to edit school config post-wizard
description: After initial setup, admins need to be able to change school settings without re-running the wizard. Create an admin/settings/school route that shows all the same fields from the wizard (branding, FACTS connection, Google config, storage) in an editable form. Include the Test Connection buttons for FACTS and storage. This is basically the wizard steps as individual tabs/sections on one settings page.
close_reason: Created admin/settings/school route with branding, FACTS, and Google Workspace sections. All wizard fields are editable with Test Connection button for FACTS. Added to admin sidebar.

title: Rewrite README with deployment guide
description:
Replace the current Epic Stack boilerplate README with a proper School ID README covering:

1. **What is School ID** - Digital ID card system for schools using FACTS SIS
2. **Features** - Employee/student IDs, QR verification, FACTS sync, optional Google integration, PDF printing, Gmail signatures
3. **Prerequisites** - Node.js 22, FACTS SIS account with API access, Fly.io account, Tigris object storage (free tier)
4. **Quick Start (Fly.io deployment)**:
   - Fork/clone the repo
   - flyctl launch (use the existing fly.toml as template)
   - Set required secrets: SESSION_SECRET, HONEYPOT_SECRET, AWS creds, FACTS keys
   - flyctl deploy
   - Visit the app → setup wizard runs automatically
5. **Environment Variables Reference** - Table of all env vars, which are required vs optional, what they do
6. **Google Workspace Setup (Optional)** - Step-by-step: create GCP project, enable APIs (Admin SDK, Gmail), create OAuth credentials, create service account, enable domain-wide delegation. Screenshots or links to Google docs.
7. **FACTS SIS API Setup** - How to get API keys from FACTS
8. **Development** - How to run locally (npm run dev, .env setup, prisma migrate)
9. **Architecture** - Brief overview: Epic Stack, React Router, Prisma/SQLite, LiteFS, Fly.io

title: Create .env.example with all configurable variables
description: Create a comprehensive .env.example file documenting every environment variable. Group by category: Core (NODE_ENV, DATABASE_PATH, SESSION_SECRET, HONEYPOT_SECRET), Storage (AWS_*), FACTS SIS (FACTS_*), Google OAuth (GOOGLE_CLIENT_*), Google Workspace (GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_*, GOOGLE_ADMIN_EMAIL), School Branding (SCHOOL_*). Mark which are required for first deploy vs configured via wizard. Note that most school-specific config can now be set via the wizard instead of env vars.

title: Update tests for generic school config
description: Update all tests that reference JPG-specific values: branding.server.test.ts (expects 'John Paul the Great Academy'), landing page tests (expects 'JPG ID System'), signature push tests (expects 'JPG Academy'), etc. Tests should use the generic defaults or mock the SchoolConfig.
close_reason: Replaced all JPG-specific values in tests: jpgacademy.org → school.org, JPG Academy → Your School, JPG ID System → School ID System (via APP_NAME import). Updated 12 test files.
